summary: Iteration 25 â€” Anomalia workflow + Inspection Templates linkage + Sobressalentes. Backend pytest 15/15 PASS covering full Anomalia lifecycle (create â†’ em_analise â†’ corrigida â†’ encerrada), invalid transition rejection (em_analise â†’ aberta = 400; encerrada â†’ anything = 400), comentarios + historico arrays in GET detail, POST /comentarios creates comment, PUT /anomalias updates descricao + emits historico entry, inspection-templates filter by tipo_equipamento, estoque GET works. Frontend Playwright validated end-to-end: login â†’ /anomalias loads â†’ create anomalia (AV-01, severidade=alta, gerar_os=true) â†’ OS auto-generated toast â†’ click card â†’ detail view â†’ Em AnĂˇlise button â†’ comment 'TEST_iter25_UI comentario' visible with 'Administrador' attribution â†’ Editar â†’ save â†’ 'TEST_iter25_UI EDITED descricao' persisted â†’ Corrigida â†’ Encerrada â†’ toast 'Status alterado para Encerrada' â†’ HistĂłrico shows all 4 transitions + edit entry â†’ back to list â†’ Encerrada filter â†’ encerrada anomalia STILL listed (NOT deleted). Filter buttons (Todas/Aberta/Encerrada) visible with counts. HistĂłrico tab on /ativos/{AV-01} shows ANOMALIA badge linked to created anomalia. Sobressalentes /sobressalentes page does NOT show 'SKU' label (uses SP-{code} format). Estoque modal label 'CĂłdigo' confirmed in iter24.

pass_fail_matrix.BACKEND POST /api/anomalias/{id}/comenta: PASS (texto persisted, visible in GET detail)
pass_fail_matrix.FRONTEND Status transition buttons (em_a: PASS â€” all 3 transitions executed via UI
pass_fail_matrix.FRONTEND Nova InspeĂ§ĂŁo modal Template-: FAIL â€” 'Templates para ALIMENTADOR VIBRATORIO' section did NOT render when modal opened from asset-detail flow via /inspecoes?ativo=â€¦ (template exists in DB and backend filter returns it correctly)

ui_bugs.issue: When opened from asset detail (URL /inspecoes?ativo={id}), the equipment-specific Templates section ('Templates para {tipo}') does NOT appear even when a matching template exists. Default mecanica/eletrica/lubrificacao tabs render instead.

action_items: Fix ModalNovaInspecao template-linked render: the useEffect on lines 1287-1307 depends on [isOpen, user, preSelectedAtivoId] but uses `ativos.find(a => a.id === preSelectedAtivoId)` from props â€” if parent InspecoesPage hasn't finished fetching `ativos` array when the modal opens with preSelectedAtivoId from searchParams, the lookup returns undefined and equipTemplates fetch is skipped. Fix by adding `ativos` to effect deps OR moving the template fetch to a separate effect that watches both `ativos` and `preSelectedAtivoId`, OR fetch templates by ativo_id from backend (let backend resolve tipo_equipamento).
Minor: 'Templates InspeĂ§ĂŁo' (sidebar) vs 'Templates de InspeĂ§ĂŁo' (page title) â€” still inconsistent from iter24.
critical_code_review_comments: Backend anomalia status state machine (server.py L2111-2116) correctly enforces transitions abertaâ†’[em_analise,encerrada], em_analiseâ†’[os_gerada,corrigida,encerrada], os_geradaâ†’[corrigida,encerrada], corrigidaâ†’[encerrada]. Returns 400 with clear message on invalid transition. Encerrada is terminal â€” good.
Backend: encerrada sets data_encerramento + encerrado_por (L2127-2129) and emits historico entry â€” good audit trail.
Backend: PUT /anomalias/{id} (L2092) emits historico entry 'Anomalia editada por â€¦' â€” good.

Frontend AnomaliasPage (App.js L4678-4921) is well-structured single-component with list/detail/edit modes and proper data-testid coverage. getNextStatuses (L4752) hides invalid transition buttons in UI even though backend also rejects â€” defense in depth.
Frontend ModalNovaInspecao template fetch race condition described above is the only blocker for feature 'Templates linked to inspection'. Quick fix: add `ativos` to effect deps array or restructure.

success_rate.frontend: 92% (12/13 critical UI flows; 1 template-linked inspection flow fails due to timing bug)

seed_data_creation: Backend test creates TEST_iter25-prefixed anomalia/template â€” anomalia is left in DB as 'encerrada' (cannot be deleted per workflow, only soft-closed). Template is cleaned up via DELETE. UI test created additional anomalia 'TEST_iter25_UI anomalia alta v2' which went through full lifecycle to Encerrada. None of these affect production data.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All anomalia workflow features work end-to-end (backend + frontend). The ONLY failing test is template-linked inspection modal when opened from asset detail flow â€” root cause is a useEffect dependency timing issue in ModalNovaInspecao (App.js L1287-1307) where `ativos.find` runs before parent's ativos array is populated. After main agent fixes this (suggest adding `ativos` to effect deps or fetching template by ativo_id from backend), re-run the Playwright section that creates a template and opens Nova InspeĂ§ĂŁo from /ativos/435593b8-a66a-4ddd-a8c6-f4bcce70d4cd. Test file at /app/backend/tests/test_iteration25_anomalia_templates.py â€” 15/15 pass. Screenshots at /app/.screenshots/iter25*.png. The Sobressalentes 'skuâ†’codigo' rename is UI-label-only (estoque modal shows 'CĂłdigo'); the backend field is still `sku` (no migration needed since old code path coexists).
rca of the issue: ModalNovaInspecao (App.js L1287-1307): when /inspecoes?ativo={id} is loaded, the modal's useEffect fires with preSelectedAtivoId set. Inside, `ativos.find(a => a.id === preSelectedAtivoId)` reads from the `ativos` prop. If the parent InspecoesPage fetches `ativos` asynchronously and the modal opens before the fetch resolves (e.g., the modal mounts immediately due to a state set on URL param parse), `ativos` is [] and the lookup returns undefined, so equipTemplates is never populated and the 'Templates para â€¦' section never renders. Mitigation: add `ativos` to the useEffect dependency array (lines 1287-1307), OR introduce a separate useEffect that watches `[ativos, preSelectedAtivoId]` to fetch templates as soon as both are available, OR have the backend resolve tipo_equipamento from ativo_id and accept ativo_id directly on /inspection-templates filter.
